summary: Nitrates primarily reduce myocardial oxygen demand by dilating veins, decreasing blood volume returning to the heart; they also affect arteries but less directly or extensively than venules. The main therapeutic effect comes from lowering left ventricular filling pressure, thereby alleviating symptoms quickly during an anginal episode.
